messages.content: grammatical form of: personal life contrasts with private life
The grammatical form of "personal life contrasts with private life" is a declarative sentence in the present tense.
grammatical structure of:personal life contrasts with private life

The grammatical structure of "personal life contrasts with private life" is Subject-Verb-Object-Object Complement. 

- Subject: personal life
- Verb: contrasts
- Object: with private life
- Object Complement: none (the sentence doesn't have an object complement, but it does have a prepositional phrase)
grammatical structure of: He lives in the public eye

The grammatical structure of "He lives in the public eye" is Subject-Verb-Object-Prepositional Phrase. 

- Subject: He 
- Verb: lives 
- Object: in the public eye 
- Prepositional Phrase: in the public eye
grammatical structure of: he deserves privacy in his personal life

The grammatical structure of "he deserves privacy in his personal life" is Subject-Verb-Object-Prepositional Phrase.

- Subject: he
- Verb: deserves 
- Object: privacy 
- Prepositional Phrase: in his personal life.
grammatical structure for: he conducted a survey about privacy

The grammatical structure of "he conducted a survey about privacy" is Subject-Verb-Object-Prepositional Phrase.

- Subject: he
- Verb: conducted
- Object: a survey
- Prepositional Phrase: about privacy.
grammatical structure of: His personal life contrasts with his private life

The grammatical structure of "His personal life contrasts with his private life" is Adjective Noun Clause - Verb - Preposition - Adjective Noun Clause.

- Adjective Noun Clause: His personal life
- Verb: contrasts
- Preposition: with
- Adjective Noun Clause: his private life.
Personal contrasts with public: grammatical structure

The grammatical structure of "Personal contrasts with public" is Adjective Noun Clause - Verb - Preposition - Adjective Noun Clause.

- Adjective Noun Clause: Personal
- Verb: contrasts
- Preposition: with
- Adjective Noun Clause: public.
contrasts with: grammatical structure ending in prepositional phrase

In "contrasts with", the preposition "with" is used to link two noun phrases. The grammatical structure of "contrasts with" can end in a prepositional phrase. Here's an example:

- His approach to teaching contrasts with traditional methods.
  - Adjective Noun Clause: His approach to teaching
  - Verb: contrasts
  - Preposition: with
  - Noun Phrase: traditional methods
  - Prepositional Phrase: with traditional methods.
the grammatical structure of: the results would emerge soon

The grammatical structure of "the results would emerge soon" is Article - Noun Subject - Auxiliary Verb - Verb - Adverb.

- Article: the
- Noun Subject: results
- Auxiliary Verb: would
- Verb: emerge
- Adverb: soon
grammatical structure of: the facts would emerge soon

The grammatical structure of "the facts would emerge soon" is Article - Noun Subject - Auxiliary Verb - Verb - Adverb.

- Article: the
- Noun Subject: facts
- Auxiliary Verb: would
- Verb: emerge
- Adverb: soon
grammatical structure of: the facts would emerge soon enough

The grammatical structure of "the facts would emerge soon enough" is Article - Noun Subject - Auxiliary Verb - Verb - Adverb - Adverb.

- Article: the
- Noun Subject: facts
- Auxiliary Verb: would
- Verb: emerge
- Adverb 1: soon
- Adverb 2: enough
grammatical structure of time to reveal the results

The grammatical structure of "time to reveal the results" is Noun - Infinitive Verb Phrase - Noun Phrase.

- Noun: time
- Infinitive Verb Phrase: to reveal
- Noun Phrase: the results.
the grammatical structure of : time to reveal the results of the survey

The grammatical structure of "time to reveal the results of the survey" is Noun - Infinitive Verb Phrase - Noun Phrase - Prepositional Phrase.

- Noun: time
- Infinitive Verb Phrase: to reveal
- Noun Phrase: the results
- Prepositional Phrase: of the survey.
